19th October, 20 I 8 Statutory Instruments
763
IN EXERc1s1-; of the powers contained in section 101 of the Mines
and Minerals Development Act, 2015, the following Rules are
made:
PART I
PRELIMINARY
I. These Rules may be cited as the MiningAppeals (Tribunal) Title
Rules, 2018.

sections.heading: In these Rules, unless the context otherwise requires- lntcrprcla­
The Mining Cadastre Department of the Mining shall provide Secretariat
sections.text:
the necessary Secretariat services to the Tribunal for the
performance of its functions under these Regulations.

sections.heading: ( 1) A person may appeal to the Tribunal against an order Institution
sections.text:
or appeal
or direction by the Minister in Form I set out in the Schedule within
thirty days of the date of the order or direction.
(2) An appeal made under these Regulations shall be lodged
with the Secretariat.

sections.heading: The Secretariat shal I, on receipt of an appeal, serve or cause Service or
sections.text:
appeal
to be served a copy of the appeal on the respondent.

sections.heading: (I) The Secretariat may, subject to any direction by the Power to
sections.text:
requi1·e
Chairperson, at any time after receiving the appeal, require the
further
appellant to furnish, in writing, such particulars as may be necessary particulars
to ensure a fair hearing.
(2) The Secretariat shall, on receipt of the particulars referred
to in subrul e ( 1 ), send a copy of the statement to the respondent.
----- -------------- -
Statutory Instruments 19th October, 20I 8
764
Answer 7. The respondent shall lodge with the Secretariat an answer
within seven working days of receipt of the appeal.
Time and 8. (I) The Tribunal shall determine the date, time and place
hearing of of hearing of an appeal.
appeal
(2) The Secretariat shall, at least fourteen days before the
hearing of the Tribunal, cause the notice of the date, time and place
of hearing of the Tribunal to be served on the appellant and
respondent in Form II set out in the Schedule.
(3) A party served with the notice referred to in subrule (2)
111ay apply, in writing, to the Secretariat for a change of the date of
hearing.
PART Ill
PROCEEDINGS OF TRIBUN/\1.
Sittings of 9. The Tribunal shall sit at a place and times that the
Tribunal Chairperson or, in the absence of the Chairperson, the Vice­
Chairperson 111ay determine, within sixty days of receipt ofan appeal.
Proceedings I 0. (1) The Tribunal shall hold its proceedings in public.
ol'Tribunal
(2) Despite subrule (]),the Tribunal may, on an application by
a party to the proceedings, direct that the proceedings be held in
camera.
(3) Subject to the other provisions of these Rules and to any
direction given by the Chairperson, the Tribunal may regulate its
own procedure.
(4) There shall preside at any proceedings of the Tribunal­
(a) the Chairperson; or
{b) in the absence of the Chairperson, the Vice-Chairperson.
Quorum 11. ( I) Three 111e111bers of the Tribunal shall constitute a
quorum, which shall include either the Chairperson or the Vice­
Chairperson.
(2) A hearing before the Tribunal shall for the purposes of
Chapter XI of the Penal Code, be deemed to be a judicial
proceeding.
(3) The determination of any matter before the Tribunal sha II
be according to the opinion of the majority of the members on the
matter, except that-
(a) in the event of an equa I ity of votes, the person presiding
at the sitting shall have a casting vote in addition to a
deliberative vote; and
19th October, 2018 Statutory Instruments 765
(b) where in any matter before the Tribunal the dispute to
be resolved is on a point of law, the decision of the
person presiding at the sitting shall prevail.
(4 ) A member who is present at a meeting or hearing of the
Tribunal at which any matter in which the member's relative or
associate is directly or indirectly interested in a private capacity is
the subject of consideration shall, as soon as is practicable after
the commencement of the meeting or hearing, disclose such interest
and shall not, unless the Tribunal otherwise directs, take part in any
consideration or discussion of or vote on any question relating to
that matter.
(5) A disclosure of interest made under this rule shall be
recorded in the record of proceedings at which it is made.
(6) The validity of any proceedings, act or decision of the
Tribunal shall not be affected by any vacancy in the membership
of the Tribunal or by any defect in the appointment of any member
or by reason that any person not entitled to do so, took part in the
proceedings.
(7) The Tribunal shall cause to be kept a record of its
proceedings.

sections.heading: Where more than one appeal is given to the Secretariat by Proceedings
sections.text:
different appellants relating to the same matter, the Tribunal may LO be
consolidated
on its own motion or on an application, in writing, by a party to the
appeal order that the appeals be consolidated.

sections.heading: (I) A person appearing as a party before the Tribunal may Appearance
sections.text:
appear in person or be represented by a practitioner. before
Tibunal
(2) The respondent shall be afforded an opportunity to be heard
in answer to the appeal.
or

sections.heading: (1 ) Where an appellant does not appear at the time and Default
sections.text:
place appointed for the hearing, the Tribunal may strike of f or dismiss appearance
'
✓- the appeal.
(2) Where the respondent does not appear at the time and
place appointed for the hearing of the appeal, the Tribunal may-
(a) proceed to hear an appeal; or
(b) adjourn the hearing on terms that the Tribunal considers
appropriate.
(3) Where proceedings are dismissed or determined under this
rule in the absence ofa party, that party may, on an application, in
writing, made by that party within fourteen days ofthe dismissal or
determination, apply to the Tribunal to set aside the dismissal or
determination and give the reasons for that party's absence from
the hearing.
766 Statutory Instruments 19th October, 20 I 8
(4) Where the Tribunal is satisfied that the reasons given by a
party under subrule (3) are sufficient to set aside the dismissal or
determination made under subrule (I) and (2), the Tribunal may set
aside that dismissal or determination.
Tribunal 15. At the hearing-
hearing
(a) the appellant shalI present the evidence which the appeal
is based;
(b) the respondent may adduce evidence to rebut the evidence
of the appellant;
(c) the appellant may address the Trubunal by way of reply
to the respondents's evidence;
(d) a party may call a witness or witnesses to adduce
evidence; and
(e) the parties may, at the conclusion of the hearing, present
oral or written submissions to the Tribunal.
Evidence 16. (I) The Tribunal may receive, as evidence, a statement,
document, informali on or other matter that may assist if effectively
consider an appeal.
(2) The Tribunal may take judicial notice ofa fact.
(3) Evidence before the Tribunal may be given orally or, if the
parties to the proceedings consent or the Tribunal so orders, by
affidavit.
( 4) The Tri bun a I may, at any stage of the proceedings make
an order requiring the personal attendance of a deponent for
examination and cross examination.
Production 17. (I) A party may, at any time during the hearing give to the
of document
other party a notice to produce any document to which the appeal
or
infomation relates which is in possession ofthat party.
(2) A pa11y to the proceedings shall, on request, produce to the
Secretariat, any document or other information which the Tribunal
may require and which is in the power of that party to produce, and
shall afford to every party to the proceedings an apportunity to
inspect those documents or copies of the documents, and to make
copies of the documents.
(3) A notice to produce any document shall be in Form Ill set
out in the Schedule.
J\clclitional 18. (I) The Tribunal may, during the hearing receive additional
inl'orma1ion
information relating to an appeal that it may consideer necessary
to enable it dispose ofa matter.
19th October, 20 I 8 Statutory Instruments
767
(2) The Tribunal may, summon witnesses, call for the production
and inspection of any book, document, record and other thing and
examine the witness.
(3) A person giving evidence or summoned to give evidence
or to produce a book, document, record or other thing before the
Tribunal, is entitled to the same privileges and immunities as if the
person were summoned to attend or were giving evidence in a civil
proceeing before a High Court.

sections.heading: (I) An interlocutory application in any proceedings shalI Interlocutory
sections.text:
be made to the Tribunal.
applications
(2) An interlocutory application shall be in writing and shall
state the title of the proceedings and the grounds on which the
application is made.
(3) Where an interlocutory application is made with the consent
of all the parties, the consent shall be in writing and signed by, or on
behalf of, the paties.
(4) Where an interlocutory application is not made with the
consent of al I the parties, a copy sha1 1, be served on the other party
or parties and the interlocutory application shall state that service
has been effected.
(5) A party who objects to an interlocutory application may,
within fourteen days, after the service of a copy on the party, send
written notice of objection to the Tribunal and a copy to the
applicant.
(6) The Tribunal shall, before making an order on an
interlocutory application, hear the parties to the application.
(7) An appeal shal I notI i e against a decision oft he Tribunal on
an interlocutory application.

sections.heading: (I) An appellant may withdrawal the appeal­ Withdrawal
sections.text:
of appeal
(a) at anytime before the hearing of the appeal; or
(b) with the consent of the Tribunal at anytime during the
hearing of the appeal, but before a final determination
by the Tribunal.
(2) An appellant who intends to withdraw the appeal shall lodge
a notice of withdrawal. in writing, to the Secretariat.
(3) The Secretariat shall serve a copy of the notice of
withdrawal referred to under subrule (2) on the parties to the appeal.
Statutmy Instruments 19th October, 2018
768
(4) The Tribunal may, where the hearing of an appeal has
conmmenced, after receiving the notice of withdrawal under subrule
(2), allow the appelant to withdraw the appeal.
Consent 21. (I) Where the parties agree on the terms of an order to
order
be made by the Tribunal, particulars of the terms agreed on shall
be in writing and signed by all the parties.
(2) The parties shall send the terms agreed on under subrule
(I) to the Secretariat.
(3) The Tribunal may, in accordance with the terms agreed on
under subrule (I), make an order in the presence or absence of the
parties.
Postponement 22. ( 1) The Tribunal may postpone the commencement of the
of hearing
hearing of an appeal or an application to withdraw the appeal.
(2) A hearing ofa n appeal may be adjourned.
PART IV
DECISION OF TRll1UN/\L
Decision or 23. The Tribunal shall render a decision on any matter within
Tribunal
sixty days after the conclusion of the hearing of the matter.
ornrnunica­ 24. The Secretariat shal I cause a decision of the Tribunal to be
tion or
communicated to the parties concerned within seven days of making
decision or
Tribunal the decision.
PART V
GENERAL PROVISIONS
Expenses or 25. The expenses and costs oft he Tribunal shall be paid out of
Tribunal
funds appropriated by Parliament for the purpose of these Rules.
Costs 26. (I) The Tribunal may make orders for costs as it considers
just having regard to the circumstances of the case.
(2) Where the Tribunal makes an order for costs, the costs
and charges in connection with any proceedings before the Tribunal
shall be the costs reasonably incurred by a party in connection with
proceeding or part of those costs thal may be determined by the
Tribunal.
(3) The Tribunal shall assess the sum to be paid in accordance
with !he scale of fees for the time being in use by the High Court in
civil cases.
(4) A party aggrieved with the assessment by the Tribunal
may apply to the Chairperson to review the decision, within fourteen
clays of the assessment.

sections.heading: A party who changes an address shall, within seven days Change or
sections.text:
of the change, by notice in writing, inform the Secretariat and the aclclrcss
other parties of the change.

sections.heading: (I) A notice or any other document required by these Rules Service of
sections.text:
notices,
to be served, may be served by delivering it personally to the person
documents.
to whom ii is addressed or may, where the Chairperson, or Vice­ etc.
Chairperson, is satisfied that the person cannot be found, or is not
in the Republic, be served on or brought to the knowledge of that
person in a manner that the Chairperson may direct.
(2) An application or communication to be made to the Tribunal
in respect of any matter before the Tribunal shal I be addressed to
the Secretariat.

sections.heading: A person aggrieved with the decision of the Tribunal may Appeals
sections.text:
appeal to the High Court.
